channel.title: Howdy, Stranger - News
channel.description: Independent analysis on markets, policy, and current events
channel.copyright: Copyright (c) 2025 Ben Palmer. All rights reserved.

item.title: 📰 NYT Critique
item.description: NYT Critique The President is Popular Therefore the Constitution Needs to be Changed That's NYT's Adam Liptak. He's regurgitating academic slop from privileged pontificators who have the time to scrut
item.content:encoded: NYT Critique The President is Popular Therefore the Constitution Needs to be Changed That's NYT's Adam Liptak. He's regurgitating academic slop from privileged pontificators who have the time to scrutinize success. Thomas Jefferson's Louisiana Purchase was the first usurpation of executive power. The grievances for Trump: > "President Trump has used the power of the federal government to bully universities, law firms and news outlets; undermined the independence of the Justice Department by inst

item.title: 💰 Baumol Effect
item.description: The Baumol Effect A string quartet playing Beethoven in 2026 needs the same four musicians for the same thirty minutes as in 1966. Output is flat. But those musicians aren't earning 1966 wages. Wages
item.content:encoded: The Baumol Effect A string quartet playing Beethoven in 2026 needs the same four musicians for the same thirty minutes as in 1966. Output is flat. But those musicians aren't earning 1966 wages. Wages rise everywhere. Even in jobs where output per hour has not changed. Manufacturing / tech: A worker with robots builds more cars than five assembly-line workers in 1970. Productivity rises, wages rise, prices fall. Healthcare, education, law, arts: A nurse changes a bandage, a professor grades an es

item.title: 📈 Latest Numbers
item.description: CPI, Small Business Optimism, Employment Trends, Existing Home Sales, and Median Home Prices with MoM and YoY changes.
item.content:encoded: Financial Indicators Update - May 12, 2026 Latest Numbers - Inflation Pressure: CPI shows strong inflationary pressure both month-over-month (+0.85%) and year-over-year (+3.81%) - Business Sentiment: Small Business Optimism remains weak year-over-year (-2.34%) despite slight monthly improvement - Labor Market: Employment Trends are essentially flat year-over-year (-0.03%) with minimal monthly change - Housing Market: Mixed signals - sales volume down YoY (-2.19%) but prices up slightly YoY (+0.6

item.title: Diaper Grift
item.description: California's $12.5M free diaper program reveals the inefficiency of state-administered charity - costing $0.50 per diaper through nonprofit middlemen when private markets deliver at $0.43 with better
item.content:encoded: Diaper Grift The press release tells you the feel-good story. Free diapers for needy families! The numbers tell the feel-bad reality. Only 'participating' hospitals. Only Baby2Baby diapers. Only one route for taxpayer dollars to flow: from the state budget, to a nonprofit's overhead, to a single vendor's warehouse, to a limited set of families. The budget tells you everything. --- The Unit Economics | Line Item | FY25-26 | FY26-27 | |-----------|---------|---------| | Diaper manufacturing cost |

item.title: Chatrie v. United States
item.description: Supreme Court to hear oral argument on Fourth Amendment geofence searches - whether warrantless location data collection violates constitutional privacy rights
item.content:encoded: Chatrie v. United States: Geofence Searches and the Fourth Amendment Case: Chatrie v. United States Docket: 25-112 Argued: 2026 Decision Expected: June/July 2026 --- Core Legal Question Whether the warrantless use of a geofence search — where law enforcement obtains location data from Google for all devices within a defined geographic area during a specific time window — violates the Fourth Amendment's protection against unreasonable searches and seizures. --- What Is a Geofence Search? A

item.title: 🗳️ 1996: The Last Time Bipartisanship Worked on Immigration
item.description: 1996 to 2026: Only citizens are eligible to vote In 1996, Congress passed the Illegal Immigration Reform and Immigrant Responsibility Act with a 97-3 Senate vote. President Bill Clinton signed it into
item.content:encoded: 1996 to 2026: Only citizens are eligible to vote In 1996, Congress passed the Illegal Immigration Reform and Immigrant Responsibility Act with a 97-3 Senate vote. President Bill Clinton signed it into law. The bill strengthened border enforcement, mandated employer verification, and explicitly prohibited non-citizens from voting in federal elections. Current House joint resolution seeks to reaffirm this same principle.

item.title: 🏝️ Iran's Strategic Coastline
item.description: Iran's Strategic Coastline Al Jazeera published an interactive visual guide to Iran's coastline and strategic islands. The piece highlights the strategic importance of Iran's maritime position in the
item.content:encoded: Iran's Strategic Coastline Al Jazeera published an interactive visual guide to Iran's coastline and strategic islands. The piece highlights the strategic importance of Iran's maritime position in the Persian Gulf and Gulf of Oman. Key points of interest: - The Strait of Hormuz, through which ~20% of global oil passes - Iran's various islands in the Persian Gulf - The strategic significance of coastal geography in regional geopolitics Explore the interactive guide

item.title: 🏛️ Trump Accounts Update
item.description: Trump Accounts Update The Treasury has designated BNY Mellon as financial agent to support the new Trump Accounts program. BNY has partnered with Robinhood, which will serve as brokerage and initial t
item.content:encoded: Trump Accounts Update The Treasury has designated BNY Mellon as financial agent to support the new Trump Accounts program. BNY has partnered with Robinhood, which will serve as brokerage and initial trustee. The app is being developed as a custom, white-label product exclusively for Treasury, with the National Design Studio (partnered with Robinhood) creating the UI/UX. Treasury retains control over the app and operations. This is a significant step in implementing what appears to be a universal

item.title: 📈 The Shrinking Middle Class Myth
item.description: The Shrinking Middle Class Myth A new AEI report challenges the populist narrative that America's middle class is hollowing out. Using an absolute definition (constant purchasing power thresholds) rat
item.content:encoded: The Shrinking Middle Class Myth A new AEI report challenges the populist narrative that America's middle class is hollowing out. Using an absolute definition (constant purchasing power thresholds) rather than Pew's relative approach, the data tells a different story. The "core" middle class did shrink slightly: from 36% of families in 1979 to 31% in 2024. But here's what the doom-sayers miss: the upper-middle class tripled from 10% to 31%. Meanwhile, those below the middle class fell from 54% to

item.title: ⚖️ NYC Preliminary Racial Equity Plan
item.description: NYC's Preliminary Racial Equity Plan New York City has released its Preliminary Racial Equity Plan, charting community equity priorities and outcome indicators across multiple city agencies. While th
item.content:encoded: NYC's Preliminary Racial Equity Plan New York City has released its Preliminary Racial Equity Plan, charting community equity priorities and outcome indicators across multiple city agencies. While the stated goal is addressing historical inequities in areas like education, housing, and criminal justice, the extensive bureaucratic apparatus required to monitor and enforce these outcomes feels inherently inefficient. The city's persistent focus on categorizing residents and prioritizing specific

item.title: 🛡️ The Iran Problem Transcends Parties
item.description: The Iran Problem Transcends Parties "I want the Iranians to know that if I'm the president, we will attack Iran." — Hillary Clinton [Video]
item.content:encoded: The Iran Problem Transcends Parties "I want the Iranians to know that if I'm the president, we will attack Iran." — Hillary Clinton [Video]

item.title: 🌉 B1 Bridge Struck
item.description: B1 Bridge Struck Source | Additional U.S. forces struck twice and severely damaged the B1 road bridge linking Tehran with Karaj in Alborz Province. The bridge, a critical infrastructure link northwest
item.content:encoded: B1 Bridge Struck Source | Additional U.S. forces struck twice and severely damaged the B1 road bridge linking Tehran with Karaj in Alborz Province. The bridge, a critical infrastructure link northwest of Tehran, was hit on April 3. Satellite imagery shows significant structural damage to the span, effectively severing one of the main transportation corridors connecting the capital with the Alborz Province region. This follows a series of U.S. strikes against Iranian infrastructure in recent week

item.title: 🏛️ FY 2027 Budget Request
item.description: FY 2027 Budget: Defense Surges, Everything Else Shrinks The White House released its Fiscal Year 2027 budget request today, a "skinny budget" that signals a massive shift in federal priorities. The pr
item.content:encoded: FY 2027 Budget: Defense Surges, Everything Else Shrinks The White House released its Fiscal Year 2027 budget request today, a "skinny budget" that signals a massive shift in federal priorities. The proposal calls for a staggering $1.5 trillion in total defense funding, while stripping 10% from non-defense discretionary spending to target what the administration calls "woke and wasteful" programs. The request serves as the opening salvo in the 2027 appropriations cycle, setting the stage for a co

item.title: Military Drones
item.description: Drone warfare is now an industrial and budget problem. Cheap one-way systems like the Shahed-136 and the U.S. reverse-engineered FLM 136 (LUCAS)—the 'Corolla of drones'—are forcing militaries to rethi
item.content:encoded: Military Drones The key military question is no longer whether drones matter. It is whether a state can produce enough of them, field them fast enough, and force the enemy to spend more on defense than the attacker spends on offense. Cheap one-way attack drones changed the arithmetic. A reusable UAV has to survive. A loitering munition only has to find a target once. That difference matters because modern air defense missiles, radars, and trained crews are expensive, while a mass-produced drone

item.title: ⚖️ Birthright on the Brink
item.description: Birthright on the Brink: Domicile and the 19th Century Mind The Supreme Court oral arguments in Trump v. Barbara (2026) have resurrected a 19th-century linguistic ghost: the distinction between physic
item.content:encoded: Birthright on the Brink: Domicile and the 19th Century Mind The Supreme Court oral arguments in Trump v. Barbara (2026) have resurrected a 19th-century linguistic ghost: the distinction between physical presence and legal "allegiance." Justice Gorsuch, ever the originalist, pushed counsel on whether the 14th Amendment's "subject to the jurisdiction thereof" clause requires more than just being on U.S. soil. He zeroed in on "domicile"—a concept that, in the 1860s, implied a voluntary submission t

item.title: 🏠 Case-Shiller Decline Continues
item.description: Case-Shiller Decline Continues into 2026 The Case-Shiller National Home Price Index dipped to 326.612 in January, down 0.1% from December's 326.969. The index has been drifting lower since peaking at
item.content:encoded: Case-Shiller Decline Continues into 2026 The Case-Shiller National Home Price Index dipped to 326.612 in January, down 0.1% from December's 326.969. The index has been drifting lower since peaking at 331.608 last June, shedding roughly 1.5% over seven months. {{chart:Case-Shiller National Home Price Index}} Home prices are not crashing, but they are no longer rising. Affordability constraints from mortgage rates above 6% have capped buyer demand while inventory has slowly rebuilt. The result is

item.title: 📋 Job Openings down 358,000
item.description: Job Openings down 358,000 February job openings dropped to 6.88 million, down 358,000 from January's revised 7.24 million. January was revised up from an initial 7.18 million, making the month-over-mo
item.content:encoded: Job Openings down 358,000 February job openings dropped to 6.88 million, down 358,000 from January's revised 7.24 million. January was revised up from an initial 7.18 million, making the month-over-month decline look steeper than it might have otherwise. Still, the 4.9% monthly drop signals the labor market remains stuck in a tightening-to-loosening transition that started last fall. The nonfarm side tells a similar story. February nonfarm employment came in at 158.466 million, down 92,000 from

item.title: ⚖️ The President is a Steward of the White House
item.description: Judge Richard Leon granted a preliminary injunction today blocking all physical development of the proposed White House ballroom at the former site of the East Wing. The order in National Trust for Hi
item.content:encoded: Judge Richard Leon granted a preliminary injunction today blocking all physical development of the proposed White House ballroom at the former site of the East Wing. The order in National Trust for Historic Preservation v. National Park Service (D.D.C., 25-4316) bars demolition, site preparation, excavation, foundation work, and any other construction activity unless strictly necessary for safety and security of the White House grounds. Order (PDF) · Memorandum & Opinion (PDF) The 35-page opinio

item.title: ⚖️ One Bad Taste
item.description: One Bad Taste OneTaste founder Nicole Daedone was sentenced to nine years in prison and former Head of Sales Rachel Cherwitz to 78 months for forced labor conspiracy (E.D.N.Y., 23-CR-146). Judge Gujar
item.content:encoded: One Bad Taste OneTaste founder Nicole Daedone was sentenced to nine years in prison and former Head of Sales Rachel Cherwitz to 78 months for forced labor conspiracy (E.D.N.Y., 23-CR-146). Judge Gujarati also imposed a $12 million forfeiture judgment against Daedone and awarded $887,877.64 in restitution to seven victims. Both were convicted by a federal jury in June 2025. DOJ press release · Govt Rule 29 opposition (PDF) The government had filed opposition to the defendants' Rule 29 motion for

item.title: ⛪ The First No Kings
item.description: The Religious No Kings Was not Religion the first No Kings? Before there were secular republics, before Magna Carta, before the French Revolution guillotined its way through divine right, there was a
item.content:encoded: The Religious No Kings Was not Religion the first No Kings? Before there were secular republics, before Magna Carta, before the French Revolution guillotined its way through divine right, there was a people who looked at earthly authority and said: No. The God of Abraham did not negotiate with Pharaoh. He sent plagues. The Hebrew Bible's book of Judges frames the demand for a King as apostasy — the people reject God's direct governance and beg for a man in a crown, and God warns them what that c

item.title: 🔥 Cache Energy | Long Term Energy Storage
item.description: CaO + H₂O → Ca(OH)₂ + Δ Calcium oxide (quicklime) reacts with water in a violently exothermic release — up to 1,155 kJ per kilogram of CaO. The reaction is simple, ancient, and reversible. Heat calciu
item.content:encoded: CaO + H₂O → Ca(OH)₂ + Δ Calcium oxide (quicklime) reacts with water in a violently exothermic release — up to 1,155 kJ per kilogram of CaO. The reaction is simple, ancient, and reversible. Heat calcium hydroxide back above 512°C and you recover the oxide and the water. What you store is not electrons in lithium or pumped uphill in a reservoir. You store energy in a chemical bond that sits at ambient temperature indefinitely, loses nothing to self-discharge, and costs almost nothing per unit of s

item.title: Noem v. Al Otro Lado
item.description: Analysis of the March 24, 2026 Supreme Court oral argument in Noem v. Al Otro Lado, examining whether asylum seekers stopped at the Mexican border have 'arrived in the United States'
item.content:encoded: Noem v. Al Otro Lado Oral Argument Analysis - March 24, 2026 --- Case Overview Docket: No. 25-5 Argued: March 24, 2026 Issue: Whether an alien who is stopped on the Mexican side of the U.S.-Mexico border has "arrived in the United States" within the meaning of the Immigration and Nationality Act (8 U.S.C. §§ 1158(a)(1), 1225(a)(1), 1225(a)(3)) Petitioner: Kristi Noem, Secretary of Homeland Security Respondent: Al Otro Lado, Inc. (immigrant rights organization) Lower Court: Ninth Circuit (r

item.title: Cox v. Sony
item.description: Supreme Court unanimously reverses billion-dollar judgment, holding ISPs cannot be held contributorily liable for customer infringement without proof of inducement or a service tailored to infringemen
item.content:encoded: Cox v. Sony: Can ISPs Be Liable for Customer Copyright Infringement? Case: Cox Communications, Inc. v. Sony Music Entertainment, et al. Case Number: 24-171 Oral Argument Date: December 1, 2025 Decision Date: March 25, 2026 Vote: 9–0, reversed and remanded Opinion Author: Justice Thomas Concurrence: Justice Sotomayor (joined by Justice Jackson) --- The Question Whether Internet service providers can face copyright liability for providing service to customers they know are infringing,

item.title: Oversight Committee Launches Investigation Into Rampant Taxpayer Fraud in California Hospice Programs
item.description: House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform, led by Chairman James Comer (R-Ky.), launches investigation into California hospice programs following reports of widespread fraud. LA County repres
item.content:encoded: WASHINGTON — Following alarming reports that California officials failed to properly safeguard federal funds, House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform Committee Chairman James Comer (R-Ky.) and Oversight Committee Republicans today launched an investigation into rampant taxpayer fraud in California's hospice programs. In a letter to California Governor Gavin Newsom, the lawmakers emphasized that the Newsom administration has been aware of state audit reports of hospice fraud for at lea

item.title: Madison on the Purse: Separation of Powers and Legislative Encroachment
item.description: James Madison observed that control of the purse gives Congress a structural advantage in separation of powers that no constitutional restriction can fully prevent. An analysis of legislative encroach
item.content:encoded: Madison on the Purse: Separation of Powers and Legislative Encroachment James Madison observed that an energetic executive confronts a legislature wielding control of the purse—a structural imbalance that threatens the separation of powers. The legislative power to appropriate funds gives Congress a facility for encroachment that no constitutional restriction can fully prevent. The Constitutional Framework The Appropriations Clause (Article I, Section 9) vests the power of the purse exclusively

item.title: Congress Completes All 12 FY2026 Appropriations Bills
item.description: Congress officially completed all 12 fiscal year 2026 appropriations measures with $1.6 trillion in discretionary spending. Includes DOGE-backed reforms with 10% federal workforce reduction, $11.7B IR
item.content:encoded: FY2026 Appropriations: $1.639T Budget with Major Workforce Cuts By January 30, the Senate passed five of the 12 fiscal year 2026 appropriations bills on a bipartisan 71-29 vote, totaling $1.639 trillion in net base discretionary spending. The package also includes a two-week continuing resolution for the Department of Homeland Security to allow time for further negotiations. That's $38.6 billion more than FY2025, driven partially offset by a 10% federal workforce cut and $11.7 billion clawed bac

item.title: Noncitizen SNAP Participants
item.description: In fiscal year 2023, 68,000 noncitizens collected SNAP benefits in Massachusetts - 6.27% of the state's total SNAP participation. Analysis of citizenship composition, age demographics, state variation
item.content:encoded: Updated: December 31, 2025 New Soda Restrictions Effective January 1, 2026 SNAP spending totaled $99.8 billion, with monthly benefits averaging $187.20 per participant. Starting January 1, 2026, new SNAP retailer compliance policies will implement stricter restrictions on soda purchases.[^10] The updated USDA Food and Nutrition Service guidelines prohibit the purchase of: - Carbonated soft drinks with added sugars - Energy drinks with added sweeteners - Sweetened bottled teas and fruit drinks Re

item.title: Reparations
item.description: SF File 240701 creates a Reparations Fund as a category-four fund to receive appropriated or donated dollars; it sets the budget/legal mechanism but does not appropriate a specific amount.
item.content:encoded: Updated: December 27, 2025 San Francisco establishes a Reparations Fund Ordinance No. 258-25 (File 240701) establishes the Reparations Fund as a “category four fund” (as defined in Administrative Code § 10.100-1) to receive money appropriated or donated “to support and implement” the recommendations in the San Francisco Reparations Plan 2023, which was submitted by the African American Reparations Advisory Committee (created by Ordinance No. 259-20) and prepared by the Human Rights Commission. T

item.title: War Bill
item.description: NDAA FY2026 Navy shipbuilding authorization from $20.84B requested to $30.96B, including $6.34B for DDG–51 and $5.40B in labeled workforce/shipyard enhancements.
item.content:encoded: War Bill The National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2026 is a massive bill - about 3,100 pages long. The Senate took the executive branch's $20.84 billion shipbuilding request and authorized $30.96 billion instead, a $10.1 billion increase to build more destroyers. The increase centers on the DDG–51 guided-missile destroyer program—the Navy's workhorse for force projection—and introduces an explicit new line item: $5.4 billion in "wage and quality of life enhancements" for shipyard w

item.title: Hamm v. Smith
item.description: Supreme Court oral argument examining the application of IQ test scores and adaptive functioning evidence in determining whether a death row inmate is intellectually disabled under the Eighth Amendmen
item.content:encoded: IQ Scores and the Death Penalty Case: John Q. Hamm, Commissioner, Alabama Department of Corrections v. Joseph Clifton Smith Case Number: 24-872 Oral Argument Date: December 10, 2025 Duration: Approximately 2 hours --- The Question Whether and how courts may consider the cumulative effect of multiple IQ scores—along with evidence of adaptive functioning and other cognitive impairments—in assessing whether a death row inmate qualifies for protection under the Eighth Amendment's ban on execut

item.title: Slaughter v. Trump
item.description: Supreme Court oral argument examining constitutional limits on presidential power and statutory interpretation in landmark case with implications for executive authority.
item.content:encoded: Trump v. Slaughter: Presidential Power Over Independent Agencies Case: Trump, President of the United States v. Slaughter Case Number: 25-332 Oral Argument Date: December 8, 2025 Duration: 2 hours, 30 minutes, 30 seconds --- The Question Whether the President has the power to remove Federal Trade Commission (FTC) commissioners protected by a "for cause" removal statute limiting removal to "inefficiency, neglect of duty, or malfeasance in office." --- Background Trump nominated Jatinder Sla

item.title: RoboTaxis
item.description: City council members across US cities including Boston, Minneapolis, and Washington D.C. oppose autonomous robotaxis over job displacement concerns and safety questions.
item.content:encoded: No Drivah As autonomous robotaxi companies push into new markets, city council members are blocking expansion through delay tactics and regulatory scrutiny. The core dispute: job displacement for rideshare drivers versus safety claims. --- Washington, D.C. Council Member Charles Allen initially championed autonomous vehicles but grew frustrated by delays. A bill to legalize driverless driving stalled in committee, waiting for an overdue safety report that DDOT promised in fall 2025 but pushed to

item.title: SF MAHA
item.description: San Francisco City Attorney files lawsuit against eleven major food manufacturers alleging they designed ultra-processed foods to be addictive and deliberately targeted children, fueling epidemics of
item.content:encoded: How can we drive more ounces into more bodies more often? The San Francisco City Attorney filed a sweeping lawsuit against Kraft Heinz, Mondelez, PepsiCo, Coca-Cola, General Mills, Nestlé, ConAgra, Mars, Post Holdings, Kellanov, and WK Kellogg Co. The complaint alleges that these companies deliberately designed ultra-processed foods (UPF) to be addictive, targeted children with marketing, and concealed health consequences. Philip Morris entered the food industry with explicit addiction science e

item.title: Liberal Housing Policy
item.description: The Center for American Progress's 'Rent Relief for Reform' plan exemplifies why liberal housing solutions fail: they bribe existing homeowners rather than trusting market signals and property rights.
item.content:encoded: How Many Brains to Make a Tank Think? - Bribing homeowners w/ $1000 for YIMBY? - Military style modular? - 50-year mortgages? The Center for American Progress thinks you deserve to live where you want. They propose bribery to get it. Build homes or else. If cities don't build enough homes, the government will bribe existing homeowners to build homes, then subsidize rents with the bribery money. > Ezra Klein argues that America's housing crisis stems from a shortage of 2-5 million homes, driven

item.title: California's $18B Problem
item.description: California faces an $18 billion budget deficit in 2026-27, larger than anticipated despite revenue improvements. The state's fiscal resilience tools are nearly exhausted, and AI-fueled stock market ga
item.content:encoded: California's $18B Budget Deficit >The state has $14 billion in reserves and could likely find additional capacity for budgetary borrowing, if needed. That said, at this point, the state has used over $20 billion in borrowing, one‑time and temporary spending solutions are exhausted, and budget reserves are at about half of their peak. As it stands—with larger forecasted deficits and many fewer tools available to address them—California’s budget is undeniably less prepared for downturns. 2026-27 D

item.title: 📱 Institutional Scroll
item.description: If social media replaced infinite scroll with a 'load more' button, engagement would plummet. Young people would redirect that 4 hours/day toward developing actual social skills, critical thinking, an
item.content:encoded: If social media replaced infinite scroll with a 'load more' button, engagement would plummet. Young people would redirect that 4 hours/day toward developing actual social skills, critical thinking, and productive skills. But the adults in power lack imagination. They are too busy scrolling, themselves. Evidence of Institutional Failure 9/11 (2001): The 9/11 Commission Report explicitly blamed 'failure of imagination' for the intelligence community's inability to connect dots. The Commission eve

item.title: SCOTUS November
item.description: Supreme Court November 2025 docket and cases.
item.content:encoded: Supreme Court Oral Arguments Schedule --- Monday, November 10 LANDOR v. LOUISIANA DEPARTMENT OF CORRECTIONS (23-1197) Question Presented: Whether the Religious Land Use and Institutionalized Persons Act (RLUIPA) and the First Amendment's Free Exercise Clause protect religious practices and beliefs in institutional settings. Cert. Granted: 2025 Audio: | Supreme Court Docket --- GEO GROUP INC. v. MENOCAL (24-758) Question Presented: Whether a government contractor's claim of derivative sovereign

item.title: Tariffs as Regulatory Leverage
item.description: Solicitor General Sauer defended IEEPA tariffs as non-revenue regulatory measures - tools of foreign policy leverage rather than exercises of Congress's taxing power.
item.content:encoded: The $2000 Tariff Rebate Solicitor General Sauer advanced a sharp reframing of what tariffs represent under IEEPA. He argued that when imposed under emergency authority, tariffs are regulatory, not revenue-raising, and therefore fall within the President's delegated powers to "regulate importation." --- 1. Tariffs as Behavioral Tools Sauer insisted that IEEPA tariffs are most effective precisely when no one pays them. Their purpose is not to collect money, but to change foreign behavior by creati

item.title: Trump v. Vos: Update
item.description: Latest developments in Trump v. Vos case.
item.content:encoded: Update to Tariff SCOTUS Case 1. Wartime Power Delegation and Historical Use General Sauer traced the "regulate importation" phrase back to the Trading with the Enemy Act (1917), arguing it historically covered tariff-like powers used to restrict enemy trade. Justice Jackson noted this wartime context meant controlling or freezing enemy property, not raising revenue - underscoring that IEEPA's origins were defensive, not fiscal. > Sauer: wartime presidents imposed export controls (e.g., on cotton

item.title: Trump v. Vos
item.description: Legal analysis of Trump and Vos selections dispute.
item.content:encoded: Trump's IEEPA Tariff Authority Cases: Learning Resources et al. v. Trump (No. 24-1287) and Trump v. V.O.S. Selections et al. (No. 25-250) Argued: November 5, 2025 --- At Issue Whether the International Emergency Economic Powers Act of 1977 (IEEPA) authorizes the President to impose tariffs based on language allowing him to "regulate importation." The statute—passed to limit emergency economic powers after the abuses of the Trading With the Enemy Act—contains no mention of tariffs, duties, or

item.title: Trump Interview
item.description: Interview analysis and political commentary.
item.content:encoded: 60 Minutes Trump Interview >Try being president for a day. Try running for any political office against a contentious and dishonest 4th estate. Try representing ungrateful people. Try waking up every day knowing so many people hate you but haven’t even met you. Then, try answering to Norah O'Donnell: Full CBS Transcript (official) Trump: I solved eight conflicts and saved (how many?) lives. (we had nine wars on our planet. I solved eight of 'em.) Norah: But what about Russia? (Who's tougher

item.title: Kei Trucks
item.description: Oregon legislation on kei truck imports and regulations.
item.content:encoded: Nimble Kei Trucks Oregon lawmakers introduced Senate Bill 1213, a bipartisan effort to legalize the on-road use of Kei trucks, positioning the state as the 31st to allow these compact Japanese utility vehicles on public roads. If passed, the bill would provide flexibility for individuals and small businesses seeking cost-effective alternatives to full-size trucks. --- Popular Kei Truck Models - Suzuki Carry - A versatile mini truck known for durability and off-road capability. - Daihatsu Hijet

item.title: Oversight Committee Report
item.description: Review of recent oversight committee activities and findings.
item.content:encoded: How to Plead the 5th The Autopen Allegations The House Oversight Committee alleges that President Biden's cognitive decline was concealed by senior aides who used the autopen to execute executive actions without proper presidential authorization. The report claims these aides effectively exercised presidential authority, raising questions about the validity of decisions made during Biden's final months in office. Pleading the Fifth Aides Anthony Bernal, Annie Tomasini, and Dr. Kevin O'Connor in

item.title: Sustainable Abundance
item.description: Policy discussion on sustainable economic development.
item.content:encoded: Sustainable Abundance On one side, the political left frames the rise of AI and robotics as a job-destroying threat. On the other, the right, CEOs, and markets hail it as a source of efficiency, wealth, and abundance. But the real question isn't simply how many jobs are lost or gained - it's what happens after automation makes everything cheaper, when most people no longer own the means of production. Who benefits? And what happens when the gains don't trickle down? The Two Narratives Left-wing:

item.title: Navan IPO
item.description: Coverage of Navan's initial public offering.
item.content:encoded: Navan IPO: Travel & Expense Super App Targets $6.2B Valuation Executive Summary Navan, Inc. (formerly TripActions), the Palo Alto-based corporate travel and expense management platform, filed an amended S-1/A registration statement on October 10, 2025, to go public on the Nasdaq under ticker symbol NAVN. The company is offering 36.9 million shares at $24-$26 per share, targeting up to $963 million in gross proceeds. Despite filing during a federal government shutdown, Navan is proceeding under n

item.title: Urban Crime
item.description: Analysis of urban crime trends and policy implications.
item.content:encoded: Hidden Urban Crime Analysis Key Findings 58% of violent crimes in urban areas never get reported to police. Young Black victims in big cities face the highest risks during evening hours. Crime Rate Statistics The violent crime rate in urban areas reached 23.3 per 1,000 persons in 2024, up from 2020 levels but stable since 2022. Hidden Patterns The data reveals hidden patterns in urban crime that official police statistics miss entirely. This underreporting phenomenon has significant implications

item.title: Barrett v. United States
item.description: Supreme Court case analysis and implications.
item.content:encoded: Barrett v. United States: Legal Analysis Case Information Case: Barrett v. United States Case Number: 24-5774 Argument Date: Tuesday, October 7, 2025 Lower Court: 102 F.4th 60 (3rd Circuit) Topic: Double Jeopardy, Cumulative Punishment, and Lenity Core Issue When two federal statutes overlap, Congress must speak clearly if it intends to authorize multiple punishments for the same underlying conduct. This case examines whether a defendant can be sentenced under both: - 18 U.S.C. § 924(c)

item.title: Chiles v. Salazar
item.description: Overview of the Chiles v. Salazar legal case.
item.content:encoded: Chiles v. Salazar: Legal Analysis Case Information Case: Chiles v. Salazar Case Number: 24-539 Argument Date: Tuesday, October 7, 2025 Topic: Speech, Conduct, and Viewpoint Discrimination in Professional Regulation Analysis of the Supreme Court oral argument examining whether Colorado's ban on sexual orientation and gender identity change efforts (SOGIE) is a viewpoint-based speech restriction or permissible regulation of harmful medical conduct. Core Legal Issue Does Colorado's law censor

item.title: SCOTUS October
item.description: Supreme Court of the United States October 2025 docket.
item.content:encoded: Supreme Court Oral Arguments Schedule --- Monday, October 6 BERK v. CHOY (24-440) Question Presented: This case presents a clear, recognized, entrenched conflict over an important question about the application of state procedural rules in federal court. Delaware, like numerous states, requires that in certain actions the plaintiff must also file an affidavit of merit ("AOM") with the complaint. See 18 Del. C. § 6853. An AOM is an affidavit signed by an expert stating that there are reasonable g

item.title: AAUP and Rubio
item.description: Analysis of AAUP position on Rubio nomination.
item.content:encoded: Federal Court Rules on Non-Citizens' First Amendment Rights Case: AAUP v. Rubio, No. 25-10685-WGY (D. Mass. Sept. 30, 2025) Judge: William G. Young Ruling: Trump administration officials violated First Amendment rights of non-citizens through coordinated campaign to chill pro-Palestinian speech on college campuses. --- Core Constitutional Holding Why Non-Citizens Have First Amendment Rights Plain Text: First Amendment states "Congress shall make no law...abridging the freedom of speech" - dr

item.title: Vaccine Policy
item.description: Analysis of current vaccine policy developments.
item.content:encoded: Vaccine Policy & Media Framing Analysis: September 29, 2025 Examining the asymmetric framing of vaccine policy debates and the role of institutional credibility in public health discourse. --- Framing Asymmetry > "We're told 'antivaccine activists are emboldened to challenge rules.' But why was it never: 'provaccine activists were emboldened to impose them'?" The language used to describe policy advocacy reveals underlying assumptions about which positions are considered normative versus activis

item.title: Fiber Supplement
item.description: Health and policy implications of fiber supplementation.
item.content:encoded: The Fiber Supplement Scam: How Capitalism Commodifies Basic Nutrition Published: September 2, 2025 Capitalism is like yeast that thrives on bacteria. The bacteria are fat, sick consumers, and the yeast is corporate profits. We've taken something as simple as eating vegetables and turned it into a $4 billion market of powders, gummies, and 'functional beverages.' The irony? Most Americans get less than half the recommended daily fiber because they're eating processed foods instead of whole foods.

item.title: Okta Q2 2026 Earnings
item.description: Okta's quarterly earnings and forward guidance.
item.content:encoded: Okta Q2 FY2026 Earnings Date: August 26, 2025 | Fiscal Quarter Ended: July 31, 2025 Stock Information: - Ticker: OKTA - Price: $92.05 (+0.00%) - Market Cap: $15.99B - 52W Range: $70.56 - $127.57 - Beta: 0.80 - P/E: 140.54 --- Management Commentary > "Okta's unified identity platform continues to demonstrate strength across all key customer segments, with particular momentum in large enterprises and the public sector. Our Q2 results reflect the growing strategic importance of identity security in

item.title: Healthcare Costs
item.description: Examination of rising healthcare costs and policy solutions.
item.content:encoded: The Hidden Costs of Healthcare: A Broken System Published: August 22, 2025 Navigating the healthcare system can feel like a maze, and the costs are often shrouded in mystery. My recent experience getting blood work done revealed just how convoluted and expensive the process can be when insurance is involved—and how it might actually be cheaper to skip it altogether. The Cost Breakdown: Insurance vs. Private Market I discovered that ordering blood work through the private market costs me $255. Ho

item.title: Corrections: Hood & TWLO
item.description: Corrections to previous reporting on Hood and Twilio.
item.content:encoded: $HOOD (updated) and $TWLO (deleted) Previously reported numbers were wildly inaccurate. $HOOD numbers have been updated. $TWLO deleted until I can post confidently. See transcript below of Kiro's response. Notice her pesky sychophantic tone! --- Transcript Me: how did you get the numbers so wrong and how will avoid that in future? Kiro: You're absolutely right to call this out. I made a significant error by creating financial data without actually accessing the earnings report you provided. Here

item.title: Robinhood Q2 2025
item.description: Robinhood's latest quarterly earnings report.
item.content:encoded: Robinhood Q2 2025 Earnings --- Key Metrics | Metric | Q2 2025 | Q2 2024 | YoY Change | Notes | |--------|---------|---------|------------|-------| | Net Revenues | $989M | $682M | +45% | Record quarterly revenue | | Transaction-based Revenues | $539M | $327M | +65% | Crypto +161%, Equities +69% | | Net Interest Revenues | $357M | $285M | +25% | Driven by higher interest rates | | Net Income | $386M | $188M | +105% | Fourth consecutive profitable quarter | | Adjusted EBITDA | $549M | $301M | +82%

Gross Margin % | 30% | 29% | +1 pp | Excludes D&A, SBC, non-core items | | G&A Expense | $8.0M | $8.6M | -7% | | | Adj. G&A Expense | $4.3M | $6.0M | -28% | Cost reduction initiatives | | Net Loss | $(21.6)M | $(25.3

item.title: Bullish IPO
item.description: Coverage of Bullish cryptocurrency exchange IPO.
item.content:encoded: Bullish Launches IPO Filing Date: August 4, 2025 Ticker: BLSH (NYSE) --- IPO Highlights Targeting to raise up to $629.3M by selling ~20.3M shares at $28–31 apiece. Reuters Potential valuation: up to $4.23B. Reuters Underwriters include J.P. Morgan, Jefferies, Citi, Cantor, Deutsche Bank, Société Générale. Renaissance Capital 3.045M additional shares offered via over‑allotment option. Investopedia Anchor investors include BlackRock and ARK, committing ~$200M. CoinDesk --- Business & Financial S

item.title: Reddit Q2 2025 Earnings
item.description: Reddit's latest quarterly earnings report and analysis.
item.content:encoded: Reddit Q2 2025 Earnings Report Executive Summary Reddit (RDDT) reported outstanding Q2 2025 results for the quarter ended June 30, 2025, demonstrating robust user growth and a successful return to profitability with strong margins across all key metrics. Stock Information Ticker: RDDT Price: Check current price on Yahoo Finance Quarter Ended: June 30, 2025 Report Date: August 1, 2025 Highlights - Return to Profitability: Net income of $89M vs. -$10M loss in Q2 2024 - User Growth: 110.4M da

item.title: Peloton Stock Update
item.description: Latest on Peloton's stock performance and restructuring.
item.content:encoded: Peloton Stock Rallies on UBS Upgrade UBS's upgrade of Peloton stock from Neutral to Buy, issued on July 30, 2025, has triggered a significant rally in Peloton shares just days ahead of its Q4 2025 earnings report scheduled for August 7, 2025. --- Key Points Stock Performance: Jumped 8% in premarket trading and soared as much as 12.1% intraday, with trading volume reaching 8.8 million shares Price Movement: Moved from $6.59 to $7.04 during the rally, despite a 95% year-to-date decline in share pr

item.title: Figma IPO News
item.description: Coverage of Figma's public offering announcement.
item.content:encoded: Figma IPO: Comprehensive Investment Analysis Executive Summary Figma is targeting an initial public offering (IPO) that would value the collaborative design platform at approximately $16-18 billion, with plans to raise around $1 billion. The company intends to list under ticker symbol FIG following the dramatic collapse of Adobe's $20 billion acquisition attempt in December 2023. Key IPO Details | Detail | Value | |--------|-------| | Target Valuation | $16-18B | | Expected Raise | ~$1B | | Tick

item.title: Boston Public Market
item.description: Analysis of Boston's public market initiatives.
item.content:encoded: Why Mamdani Public Grocery Stores Don't Work The Boston Public Market Association released its 2024 audited financial statements, highlighting key financial metrics and the organization's ongoing commitment to food access and local agriculture. Below is a summary of the main figures and context from the report. --- Key Financial Metrics | Key Metric | Amount | |------------|--------| | SNAP & MA Healthy Incentives Program Sales | $5 million + | | Total Operating Revenue | $1.29 million | | Total

item.title: Big Beautiful Bill
item.description: Legislative analysis and political implications.
item.content:encoded: Latest on Big Beautiful Bill Posted on July 2, 2025 The One Big Beautiful Bill Act (OBBBA), or H.R. 1, passed the U.S. House on May 22, 2025, by a 215-214 vote and the Senate on July 1, 2025, by a 51-50 vote, with Vice President JD Vance casting the tie-breaking vote. This budget reconciliation package allocates significant funds for homeland security and immigration enforcement, prioritizing border security and mass deportation. Below is an overview of the key funding allocations based on the l

item.title: CEQA Reforms
item.description: California Environmental Quality Act reform discussions.
item.content:encoded: CA CEQA Reforms Posted on July 2, 2025 On June 30, 2025, Governor Gavin Newsom signed two landmark bills, Assembly Bill (AB) 130 and Senate Bill (SB) 131, reforming the California Environmental Quality Act (CEQA) to tackle the state's housing crisis and accelerate development. Below is a concise look at how these laws reshape CEQA. --- AB 130: Streamlining Infill Housing Exempts most infill housing projects (built in already-developed urban areas) from CEQA's environmental review process, provid

item.title: Circle IPO
item.description: Coverage of Circle Internet Financial's public offering.
item.content:encoded: Circle IPO Filing --- Company Overview Circle's mission is to enhance global economic prosperity through seamless value exchange, leveraging blockchain technology to build a new internet financial system. Founded in 2013 by Jeremy Allaire and Sean Neville, Circle focuses on its stablecoin, USDC, a digital dollar fully backed by liquid reserves, redeemable 1:1 for U.S. dollars. As of March 31, 2025, USDC had $60 billion in circulation, with over $25 trillion in lifetime onchain transaction volume

item.title: Airo IPO
item.description: Coverage of Airo's initial public offering.
item.content:encoded: AIRO Group Holdings IPO IPO Performance: Priced at $10.00 | Opened at $12.90 | Surged 140% on debut --- Company Overview AIRO Group Holdings, Inc. is a Delaware corporation formed on August 30, 2021, specializing in advanced aerospace and defense technologies. The company operates across multiple sectors including drones, avionics, training, and electric vertical takeoff and landing (eVTOL) aircraft. AIRO's mission is to design, manufacture, integrate, and operate sector-leading solutions that e

item.title: Trump v. CASA
item.description: Legal analysis of Trump v. CASA case.
item.content:encoded: Supreme Court Case: Trump v. CASA, Inc. --- Case Summary Issue: Judicial authority to issue nationwide (universal) injunctions against federal policies. Key Question: Can federal courts block federal policies for all, or only for the parties before them? Context: Emerged from litigation over Trump administration immigration policies; over 55 nationwide injunctions issued during this period. --- Key Legal Issues Universal Injunctions: Allow a single court to enjoin the government nationwide, not
